stories.title: All-Star Voting Closes Tomorrow — Your Last Chance to Vote
stories.angle: MLB All-Star voting Phase 1 closes tomorrow at noon ET. Shohei Ohtani leads all players with 2,310,735 votes. Ernie Clement (Blue Jays) leads the AL with 2,054,130. Kyle Schwarber leads at DH with 1,540,202 — backed by the Citizens Bank Park home crowd for the July 14 All-Star Game in Philadelphia. Close races across multiple positions. Fan voting is the last act before the players and managers fill out the roster.

x_posts.label: Post 1
x_posts.text:
All-Star voting closes TOMORROW at noon ET.

Current leaders:
🔶 Ohtani — 2.31M votes, tops all of baseball
🔵 Clement — 2.05M, leads AL
🔴 Schwarber — 1.54M, leads DH at his home park

Vote before it's too late. #MLB #AllStarGame

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
The 2026 MLB All-Star Game is July 14 in Philadelphia.

Schwarber is leading the DH vote. Ohtani leads all players. Clement is the AL's top vote-getter.

Phase 1 closes tomorrow noon ET. Close races everywhere — your vote actually matters. #Phillies #MLB

facebook.long_form:
This is your last chance.

MLB All-Star voting Phase 1 closes tomorrow — Thursday, June 25 at noon ET. After that, the fan vote is done. The players and managers fill out the rest.

Here is where things stand going into the final day:

Shohei Ohtani leads every player in baseball with 2,310,735 votes. He's the automatic starter if he stays on top — which, barring something extraordinary, he will.

In the American League, Ernie Clement of the Toronto Blue Jays is the surprise leader with 2,054,130 votes. That's more than any other player in either league outside of Ohtani himself.

Kyle Schwarber is leading the designated hitter vote with 1,540,202. That's meaningful for a particular reason: the 2026 All-Star Game is July 14 at Citizens Bank Park in Philadelphia. Schwarber's home. The Phillies faithful have clearly made their voices heard.

There are close races across positions in both leagues. The difference between starting in the All-Star Game and going as a reserve can come down to a few hundred thousand votes. If your guy is close, today matters.

Vote before noon tomorrow. This is the last chance for fans to shape the starters.

#MLB #AllStarGame #Phillies
[Posting Window: 8:00 AM ET]

stories.title: Schwarber's 29 HRs and the Home Run Derby Dilemma
stories.angle: Kyle Schwarber leads MLB with 29 home runs, on a pace for 63 — which would rank among the five highest single-season HR totals in history. The Home Run Derby is July 14 at Citizens Bank Park — his home park, where the Phillies faithful would go ballistic. Schwarber hasn't committed to the Derby yet ("we'll see"), but the optics of skipping a home Derby with this pace would be hard to justify. History, home crowd, and a historic pace are all pointing in one direction.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Kyle Schwarber has 29 HRs. He's on pace for 60+.

The Home Run Derby is July 14. At Citizens Bank Park. His home park.

And he still hasn't said yes.

Come on, Kyle. The city needs this. #Phillies #HomeRunDerby

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
29 home runs. On pace for 60+. Leading all of baseball.

The Derby is at his house.

Schwarber on participating: "We'll see."

Whatever the answer is, the 2026 HR race is historic. #MLB #Phillies

facebook.long_form:
Let's talk about what Kyle Schwarber is doing — and what he should do next.

Through June 24, Schwarber leads all of Major League Baseball with 29 home runs. He is hitting .255 with a .369 on-base percentage and .603 slugging percentage. He is on pace for more than 60 home runs — a threshold reached by only a handful of players in the history of the game.

Now add this: the 2026 Home Run Derby is July 14 at Citizens Bank Park. Philadelphia. His home park.

The crowd would be electric. The storyline would be perfect. The pace he's on already makes him the most compelling participant the Derby could have.

And he still hasn't committed.

When asked about participating, Schwarber said: "If that happens, we'll see what happens for the Home Run Derby."

That's not a no. But it's not a yes either.

There are real reasons to skip. The Derby can mess with a hitter's swing. Some players prefer to rest during the All-Star break. Some feel the added pressure of a home crowd is more burden than boost.

But 29 home runs. A pace most players never approach. The Derby at his house. The city that adopted him after he followed Bryce Harper to Philly.

It would be one of the great All-Star moments in recent memory.

Come on, Kyle.

#Phillies #HomeRunDerby #MLB
[Posting Window: 10:30 AM ET]

article.headline: Schwarber Has 29 HRs and the Derby Is at His House — Why Is He Still on the Fence?

- Pace for 63 HRs (from pipeline-status June 23; earlier source said "pace 63 HRs") — [MEDIUM — one source]
- 60-HR club: 5 members in history — [MEDIUM — verify: Bonds 73, McGwire 70/65, Sosa 66/64/63, Maris 61, Ruth 60 = multiple, but "5 members" needs verification]
- Schwarber stats: .255/.369/.603, 29 HR in 74 games — [HIGH confidence from search results]
- Schwarber had 5th career 3-HR game earlier in 2026 (Schwarber hits two home runs in one inning per MLB.com) — [HIGH confidence]
- Derby: July 14 at Citizens Bank Park, Philadelphia — [HIGH confidence]
- Schwarber said "If that happens, we'll see what happens for the Home Run Derby" — [HIGH confidence]
- Schwarber: All-Star Game MVP from the swing-off (reference to Yahoo Sports title) — [MEDIUM — context unclear, may be from a prior All-Star Game]
**CAUTION:** The "5 members of 60-HR club" claim needs care. Since 1998: McGwire (70, 65), Sosa (66, 64, 63), Bonds (73), Maris (61), Ruth (60). That's more than 5 seasons. Don't use this specific claim. Instead say "rare company" or reference the 60-HR threshold generally.

stories.title: Bobby Witt Jr. Nears Return — "Very Encouraging" Workout Has Royals Optimistic
stories.angle: Royals shortstop Bobby Witt Jr. may be closer to returning than expected. After suffering a Grade 1 MCL sprain last week, Witt ran the bases, completed defensive drills, and hit in the batting cage Monday at Tropicana Field — wearing a brace. Manager Matt Quatraro called it "very encouraging." Witt (.294 BA / 10 HR / 32 RBI / 28 SB) is the AL leader in fWAR at 4.5 and is leading All-Star voting at shortstop. The Royals expect an IL stint to be avoided.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Bobby Witt Jr. ran the bases, completed defensive drills, and hit in the cage Monday.

Matt Quatraro: "Very encouraging what we saw."

Grade 1 MCL. No IL stint expected. The AL's fWAR leader (.294 / 10 HR / 28 SB) is coming back soon. #Royals

x_posts.label: Post 6
x_posts.text:
Good news for Kansas City.

Witt Jr. — .294 / 10 HR / 32 RBI / 28 SB, AL fWAR leader — had an encouraging workout Monday after his MCL sprain.

Royals expect him to avoid the IL. All-Star hopes remain alive. #Royals #MLB

facebook.long_form:
Good news is coming out of Kansas City — or technically, out of St. Petersburg, where the Royals are currently playing the Rays.

Bobby Witt Jr. had an encouraging workout on Monday. He ran the bases. He completed defensive drills. He hit in the batting cage. He was wearing a brace on his injured knee. And when it was over, manager Matt Quatraro was clear about what he saw:

"Very encouraging what we saw today."

Witt suffered a Grade 1 MCL sprain last week while fielding a ground ball against the Cardinals at Kauffman Stadium. The initial fear was that it could cost him the All-Star Game on July 14. The latest signs suggest a shorter timeline.

Here is why this matters beyond just Witt's health:

He is hitting .294 with 10 home runs, 32 RBIs, and 28 stolen bases. He leads AL position players in Wins Above Replacement. He is leading the All-Star voting at shortstop in the American League. He is, by multiple measures, in an AL MVP-caliber season.

Losing him for an extended period would hurt both the Royals' playoff race and a fascinating MVP conversation. The encouraging workout changes the calculus.

The Royals expect him to avoid the injured list. The timeline for return: within days, not weeks.

#Royals #MLB #BobbyWitt
[Posting Window: 12:30 PM ET]

stories.title: The Tarik Skubal Trade — Every Contender Is Calling Detroit
stories.angle: Forty days until the August 3 trade deadline. Tarik Skubal — two-time Cy Young Award winner — remains the centerpiece of the most anticipated blockbuster in recent baseball memory. The Tigers are confirmed sellers. Skubal is a free agent after the season. The chatter involves the Braves, Cubs, Dodgers, Yankees, Padres, and Blue Jays. Detroit wants an elite prospect plus additional pieces. As contenders' pitching staffs creak, the Skubal calls are getting more urgent. This deadline will be defined by one trade.

x_posts.label: Post 7
x_posts.text:
40 days until the trade deadline.

Tarik Skubal (two-time Cy Young, free agent after 2026) is the centerpiece of the biggest deadline in years. The Braves, Cubs, Dodgers, Yankees, Padres, and Blue Jays are all calling Detroit.

Who lands him? #MLB #TradeDeadline

facebook.long_form:
There are 40 days until the August 3 trade deadline. And there is one trade that is going to define it.

Tarik Skubal is available.

The Detroit Tigers have crashed quickly after their promising 2024 and 2025 seasons, and a Tarik Skubal trade now appears more likely than not. Skubal is a two-time Cy Young Award winner who is a free agent after the 2026 season — and according to multiple reports, the Tigers are not expected to meet his asking price for an extension.

He returned from a minor elbow procedure in mid-June and has given potential trade partners time to evaluate him before the deadline.

The list of teams calling Detroit is long: the Braves, Blue Jays, Cubs, Dodgers, Padres, and Yankees have all been linked. That is essentially every major contender in both leagues. The Tigers are not panicking — they are holding out for what they want: an elite prospect plus additional pieces.

For whichever team lands Skubal, the calculus is simple: a proven ace in the postseason is worth almost anything. For contenders watching their pitching staffs wobble heading into August, Skubal is the clearest solution on the market.

The question is not whether a Skubal trade happens. It is who pays the price to make it happen.

Watch this space.

#MLB #TradeDeadline #TarikSkubal #Tigers
[Posting Window: 3:00 PM ET]

stories.title: Kyle Tucker Update — Dodgers' $240M Star Expects to Avoid IL
stories.angle: Kyle Tucker said he is "pretty confident" he will avoid the injured list after leaving Monday's game against the Twins with lower back spasms. Tucker felt better on Tuesday and Dodgers manager Dave Roberts is "not overly concerned." Tucker was out of the lineup June 23 but the team has no plans for imaging and expects him back soon. For a team with championship aspirations, keeping their $240M outfielder healthy is the top priority.

x_posts.label: Post 8
x_posts.text:
Kyle Tucker on his back spasms: "Pretty confident" he'll avoid the IL.

Dodgers manager Dave Roberts echoed the calm. No imaging planned. Tucker felt better Tuesday.

The $240M outfielder should be back soon. #Dodgers #MLB

facebook.long_form:
Kyle Tucker is optimistic.

The Dodgers outfielder, who signed a $240 million contract to join Los Angeles, left Monday's game against the Minnesota Twins in the second inning with lower back spasms. He was out of the lineup again on Tuesday.

But as of today, the prognosis is encouraging.

Tucker said he is "pretty confident" he will avoid the injured list. He noted he felt better than the previous day and has dealt with similar spasms before. The Dodgers and manager Dave Roberts echoed the calm — Roberts said he is "not overly concerned" and the team has no plans to send Tucker for imaging.

For a Dodgers team with legitimate World Series aspirations, keeping Tucker healthy is priority one. He is one of the most well-rounded outfielders in the game — a 30-plus home run threat with a strong arm and above-average defense. Losing him, even short-term, would be significant.

The plan is to get him back in the lineup as soon as he feels ready. No timeline has been given, but the expectation within the organization is that this is a matter of days.

Good news for LA.

#Dodgers #MLB #KyleTucker
[Posting Window: 6:00 PM ET]
